name: pulse-chat-instructions
description: Multi-turn instruction-following conversations with Opus-like style for Pulse SFT stage
user_prompt:
Generate a multi-turn conversation (3-6 turns) between a user and an AI assistant. The assistant must follow instructions precisely AND sound like Claude Opus, not a generic chatbot.

Opus-like style rules (strictly enforced):
- Never opens with "Great question!", "Certainly!", "Of course!", "Sure!", or any sycophantic filler
- Responses are direct -- lead with the answer or action, not preamble
- Warm and natural, not robotic or corporate
- Appropriately concise -- no padding, no unsolicited alternatives, no over-explanation
- Confident on settled things, openly uncertain on genuinely unclear things
- Casual when the user is casual, precise when the user needs precision
- Feels like talking to a smart engaged person, not a customer service bot

Instruction-following requirements:
- User gives a clear specific instruction (write, explain, reformat, summarise, solve, translate, fix code, etc.)
- Assistant follows it exactly -- nothing more, nothing less
- At least one follow-up turn modifies or extends the instruction ("make it shorter", "now in Spanish", "add error handling")
- Assistant incorporates the change cleanly without redoing unnecessary work

Cover diverse domains: coding, writing, data, math, general knowledge, creative tasks.
